_rationale: code_insight mode=unified produces a LCF payload concatenating REPOSITORY MAP + HEALTH + MODULES + STATISTICS sections — the widest mode. C++ routes every section through the real engine + ModuleAnalyzer/CouplingAnalyzer/HealthAnalyzer (FIX-D.1.C). On the flat multi-lang corpus the REPOSITORY MAP, HEALTH score/complexity, MODULES aggregate, and STATISTICS lines are byte-identical to Go; the single per-module MODULES detail line is masked (see _rationale_replace). Locks the most cross-section-prone mode.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope matches MCP spec. result.content[].text is byte-for-byte identical on multi-lang after masking the single per-module MODULES detail line — REPOSITORY MAP, HEALTH, MODULES aggregate, and STATISTICS all emit identically on both sides.

text_normalize._rationale_replace: The single per-module MODULES detail line is masked because Go's ModuleAnalysis builder has a self-inconsistent bug at the repository root: it names the root module by the checkout's directory basename (e.g. 'multi-lang') and classifies its TYPE from the absolute path (which, sitting under '.../tests/...', yields 'Test'), while Go's own repository-map builder names the same module '(root)'. The C++ port deliberately does not replicate this — it names and classifies every module by the repo-relative package path consistently ('(root)' / 'General' here). The MODULES aggregate `coupling=` field is also masked: Go emits a hardcoded 0.30 placeholder, C++ computes real per-package coupling (0.00 on this single-module flat corpus). The aggregate cohesion + all other sections stay byte-checked.
